description:
The Wynn's Safari “The Game” is based on the annual Sydney to Darwin marathon.  Players Drive their off road vehicles from Sydney to Darwin stopping at check points and tacking short cuts along the way.  
Really a glorifying snakes and ladders used as a promotional product for Wynn's fuel additives.  
No copyright or publisher shown on box, board or rules.

The Battle of Austerlitz was fought on December 2, 1805. Napoléon's Grand Armée concluded a brilliant campaign by defeating a combined force of Russian and Austrian troops. Napoleon lured the two allies to their destruction by feigning weakness, then crushing their hasty advance with a left wing envelopment. It is considered Napoléon's greatest victory.

Designed by Bruce McFarlane, Austerlitz modifies the game system of in Gettysburg: Badges of Courage to reflect Napoléonic combat. It can be played with two or three players.

FRIENDO™ is a game that challenges your ability to guess how others will react in a given situation.

For every sitting of a game, one story, along with 5 related questions, is used. This item is known as a "Case 'N Questions™".

Total Score = Points x Friendo Chips.

The objective of FRIENDO™ is to maximize Total Score.  To do that, you must be able to 1)correctly guess how others answer their questions AND 2)partner-up with somebody and correctly guess each other's answers.

If you stay single, (i.e. without a partner), you get no Friendo Chip, and thus, will always have a Total Score of zero.

Game is over when either 1)all the answers (20 if there are 4 players) have been guessed or recalled or 2)when, as a result of players going bankrupt of Points, only 3 players are left on the board.  There must be at least 4 players for a game to continue.  Then, whoever has the most Total Score wins.

Free online multi player game available at www.friendo.com.  Click "Play Online".

An expansion for Zombie Plague. This expansion introduces legless/torso Zombies into the game. Although slower moving than normal Zombies (which isn't saying much) they have the ability to latch onto humans and are replaced on a two for one basis.

Expands:

    Zombie Plague
A book which lets kids play game of multiplication, division, fractions, probability, estimation, mental math, and more.
family: Admin: Book entries that should be split

description: A book which lets kids play games which teach: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, time, money, fractions, geometry, and more.

boardgame: The All New Brit Quiz Game
description:
One (the third(?)) in a series of simple quiz games that require, as the name suggests, knowledge of British trivia...

Players take turns to roll a die and move that number of spaces round the board. The player then tries to answer a question determined by both the colour of the space he or she lands on, and the actual number rolled on the die. If the question is answered correctly the player moves forwards an additional three spaces as a reward. There is no penalty for getting the answer wrong.

The question categories are:

Orange - History & This And That
Yellow - People
White - Places
Green - Showbiz
Blue - Best of British luck
Red - Sport
Brown - Up The Creek

The questions for Sport, History & This & That, People, Places and Showbiz all require straight answers to a straight forward question.

The questions for the Best of British luck are in a different format with 6 different types available - Multiple Choice, Odd Man Out, Missing Letters, Anagrams, Whereabouts, and Draw - with the last four of these categories being conducted against a 30 second timer.

'Up The Creek' is a special category that asks the player to perform an activity rather than answer a question. If the player fails to carry out the activity to the satisfaction of the other players, or refuses to even try, he or she has to move backwards three spaces.

The winner is the first player to complete a circuit of the track around the board.

The second in a series of quiz games that require, as the name suggests, knowledge of British trivia...

Game play is as for the first edition:

Players take turns to roll a die and move that number of spaces round the board. The player then tries to answer a question determined by both the colour of the space he or she lands on, and the actual number rolled on the die. If the question is answered correctly the player moves forwards an additional three spaces as a reward. There is no penalty for getting the answer wrong.

'Up The Creek' is a special category that asks the player to perform an activity rather than answer a question. If the player fails to carry out the activity to the satisfaction of the other players, or refuses to even try, he or she has to move backwards three spaces.

The winner is the first player to complete a circuit of the track around the board.

Question categories are slightly changed from the first edition, and the questions are new:

Orange - History & This And That
Yellow - People
White - Places
Green - Showbiz
Blue - Animals & Plants
Red - Sport
Brown - Up The Creek

This book contains complete information and reviews of over 150 of the most popular wargames on the market in 1980. Included are data on publishers, prices, playing time, packaging, game scale, size, balance, key features, playability, rules, realism and degree of complexity for over 150 wargames.

The book features an introductory wargame: Kassala.

description:
Check out what the Chinook winds have blown our way ... tumbling down the east coast of the rocky mountains and tangled among the the tumbleweeds, Lethbridge On Board got caught in a barbed wire fence and landed in the city!

Lethbrige On Board is a '-opoly style' property owning and trading game themed after prominent landmarks in the city of Lethbridge, Alberta, Canada.

From the box:
'The board is a bird's eye view of George Street, Charlotte Square and St. Andrews Square in the New Town of Edinburgh.'
'Players must first park their car and then visit up to eight shops or businesses in the area. However, many hazards await them - Parking Meters, Traffic Wardens, Endless delays and Diversions'
The idea is to 
1) park your car and put money in the meter. This determines how many turns you have before you must return to your car.
2) visit the shops as determined by a random card allocation
3) return to your card before your money runs out (or the Traffic Warden gets you!)
4) Repeat until all your errands are finished and you drive away.

boardgame: BUX: the matchin' cash-in card game
description:
The Differences: No cards are ever taken into your hand. Your cards are kept on the table in front of you, side by side with the symbol side up. These cards are called your 'spread'.

Your Objective: Have the most "Bux" chips when the game ends.

The Big Idea: Buy or swap cards to add to your spread. From your spread, make matching 3-card sets to sell back to the Bank for chips.

Start with 3 face-up cards on the table and 200 chips.

On your turn, make any one of three moves:
    1. draw 3 cards and auction them off to the highest bidder, OR
    2. swap away an unprotected card from another player, OR
    3. cash in matching 3-card sets for more chips.

You're out-bluffing and out-bidding others to get the cards you need and prevent them from getting cards they need. You're swapping away others' unprotected cards to build your value and ruin theirs.

Note: Revised Official rules (2018) available from Publisher's website at https://buxthegame.com/pages/rules

POINT ZERO - The UNICEF Strategy Game is a roll and move and tile placement board game simulating the development of an under-developed region of the world by UNICEF.

The board is divided into four regions, one for each player, representing a village in the African Bush, the Amazon Basin, the Sahel Belt or South East Asia. An information card for each area provides details of the kind of assistance required and the types of trained personnel required to provide it - this varies from region to region.

Players take it in turns to move their "Community Leader" around the outer circuit of the board the number of spaces determined by the roll of either one or two dice. In so doing the players collect (and on occasion lose) a variety of Community workers, Medics, Technicians, Teachers and Social workers. The directions in the spaces also initiate good or bad events in the region.

At various points on the board these helpers can be used to develop part of their village. The player developing the Sahel Belt, for example, will need to construct a safe water supply, Milk Processing Plant, Health Care Center, School etc... Construction requires then right compliment of experts and may also cost points which are earned through completing circuits of the board.

The construction undertaken and equipment purchased is represented on the board by a series of jig-saw pieces which are placed within a frame that is positioned over the game board. The pieces can be added into the frame in any order, but successful redevelopment of a village will require a total of 11 pieces.

The winner is the player that is first to have successfully reconstructed his or her village by covering the developing area with 11 reconstruction pieces.
Billed as an 'exciting game of strategy and imagination' but looks and plays a lot like Battleship.

In this game, you are a traveling sales person who is sent around the country trying to make sales.  It is a basic roll and move game, with everyone choosing a different "home territory" (starting place).  You must pay for expenses along the way, and the winner is the person who makes the most money.

First to Five is a fun, challenging Go-Moku game of tactical moves and counter moves. The object of the game is to be the first player to place five marbles in a formation like the "five" on a die. Players take turns placing marbles and may make the "5" pattern any size on the playing board as long as all the marbles are equally spaced.

boardgame: White Ensign: A Supplement for Harpoon Covering the Royal and Commonwealth Navies from 1960 to Present Day
description:
There was a time when the sun never set on the British Empire. The sinews that kept the empire together were the ships of the Royal Navy, and later, those of the Commonwealth nations. Today, just like it was in the past, whenever those ships sail, one can see the White Ensign.

White Ensign discusses each conflict in which Commonwealth naval forces were involved from 1960 to the present day. These include: Yemen 1960-67, Borneo 1962-66, Rhodesia 1965-66, Iceland 1972-73, Falklands 1977, Desert Shield/Storm 1990-91, Kuwait 1961 (Operation Vantage), East Africa 1966, Suez 1966-73, Cyprus 1974, Falklands 1982, Adriatic 1993-96.

Perhaps the most comprehensive study of the modern Royal Navy outside of official publications and a valuable reference book in its own right, White Ensign is meant to be used as a supplement to Harpoon4 and requires the use of Harpoon4 rules to derive the maximum value from it. All ships, from carriers down to patrol/fast attack craft, as well as ships taken up from trade (STUFT) during the Falklands War are included in the ship data annex, as are all aircraft that were or could have been used in a naval environment. Also included is an extensive bibliography which includes official sources and internet sites.

Children's game for Ages 4 - 10.  Players take turns stacking blocks at the end of the playing surface.  Eventually the weight of the blocks will cause the rubber-band loaded billy goat to launch into the wall of blocks, knocking them down.  The player who was putting a brick on the wall when the billy goat was triggered is now out of the game.  The goat is reset, and play starts over.  The last player remaining wins.

(From the description on the games box)

Have you ever... been to a live Pro-Wrestling event?

Do you... own leather pants?

These questions and over 300 more are included in this hilarious look at the loser in all of us. One by one, players are eliminated as they share their humorous histories.

Spice up any gathering with LOSER!

A Mystery Theme Party for 6 to 20 Players.

Get Ready for an evening of fun, laughter and suspense as you and your guests race to save the world.

This game includes both an adult and child version of the game.

Great for Family Get Togethers, Neighborhood Fun, Birthday Parties, etc.

The only thing that's "impossible" is not having fun!

Dr. Jones has stolen top-secret information, which he plans to publish on the Internet. Your mission, should you decide to accept it, is to travel as teams to five foreign countries and decode the secret information in each one. Teams follow a travel map to different country locations. As always, should any of your team be caught, the host will disavow any knowledge of this mission.

A children's dexterity game where players use a magnetic wand to push a magnetic bee around a track on the board.  Players must be careful not to get the want too close to the bee or he will "sting" them (lift up off the board and touch the wand) and they will lose their turn.

A children's game in which players spin the spinner to determine which section of the pivoting game board they must place their plastic goose piece on.  Players continue adding pieces until one side of the board weighs sufficiently more than the other and tips over, spilling the geese into the pot below.

boardgame: Games: American Boxed Games and Their Makers
description:
From the Publisher:
In America, games have become a part of our national heritage. Their brightly lithographed boxes and themes based on morals, nursery rhymes, history, television and geography have reflected the growth of our country for over 150 years. Games: American Boxed Games and Their Makers profiles and prices thousands of games manufactured from 1822-1992. Histories of hundreds of manufacturers, including "the big four" (McLoughlin Brothers, Milton Bradley, Selchow & Righter, and Parkers Brothers); collecting guidelines for both experts and novices; and advice on determining the relative value of a game will help you know whether to tiddle or wink.

This 197-page book includes short histories of many American game companies, along with many pictures from Whitehill's huge collection including 10 color pictures.  Original retail price $19.95.

description:
This 159-page book is a pictorial of boards and a few playing pieces from games in the New York Historical Society's collection.  Really a coffee table book, there are very short descriptions of the games.

From the back cover:
The Games We Played: The Golden Age of Board and Table Games brings together more than one hundred eye-catching examples of America's most spectacular games. A veritable encyclopedia of late nineteenth- and early twentieth- century games, this playful visual survey provides a revealing look at the social values and preoccupations of the culture that produced them. Organized around themes such as moral education, city life, sports, war, and travel, this book casts its entertaining subject matter in a fascinating new light, for avid and occasional game players alike.

description:
Two players each have a board with a mirror at one side and two two-sided pieces with a red and yellow pattern on them. Each round, one of the cards is turned face up, showing a pattern that can be made with the two pieces and their image in the mirror.

Simultaneously, both players try to create the pattern on the card with their two pieces. The first player who manages this gets the card, which counts as a victory point. When the deck is exhausted, the player with the most cards wins.

For solitaire play, the rule book includes extra patterns using all four pieces.

boardgame: What Shall I Wear?: A  Fashion Game for Girls
description: The goal of this game is to be the first player to be dressed for two invitations.  Players roll dice and move around the board to different sections, the "Daytime Boutique," "Sports Fair," and "Formal Salon" collecting parts of different outfits.

Print and play game with a monster theme.

From the website:

"In this game, you're one of four Phantoms racing around a crumbling Druidic Temple, hoping to be the
first to take control of the VORTEX (which controls all space and time, binds the universe together, and
picks up FM radio like you wouldn't BELIEVE)! You'll have to battle the fearsome Sentinels that guard
these ruins, and your opponents will be attacking you with a variety of horrible creatures. This game's
very easy to learn to play, so phantoms of all ages can enjoy it. And once a few Phantom's start
hurling savage ravenous monstrosities at each other... well, it's sure to be a fun time!"

From the website:
"In this game, you're one of four Banshees racing to be the first to collect five skulls from the surrounding tombs. Standing on the red-stained stones (I'm not certain why they're stained red, but I suspect it's leakage from the tombs), you perform the mystic ritual of 'Spinning the Bones' to overcome the magic spells of the Stone Sentinels (which keep the tombs closed). Next you peer inside the tomb, hoping to find a skull to steal (the skeletons don't mind because then they're able to watch the race, which they enjoy greatly), and hoping not to find a Vampire (because you would then have to give the Vampire a skull for having disturbed him). Racing away with a skull, you think you might WIN this time...and then you see the horrible Zombie lurching towards you, intent on eating...that's right....your SKULL!!!

(MIDNIGHT BANSHEES has a spinner under the gameboard,
so you never know which tomb might contain a Vampire!)"

Game with an optional 3-D staircase component.  Players move to rescue King Arthur from the dungeon, where he is in one of four cells.  The other three cells contain monsters, which may be defeated with matching colored potions collected from the castle above.

From Publisher:

Players avoid the Black Knight and make evil monsters vanish as they appear from behind cell doors.  The true Merlin is found when a player is the first to rescue King Arthur from the dungeon.

A great first game for girls and boys - no reading required!

2-4 Players, Ages 4 - 11

Contents:

1 Cardboard Stone Staircase

description:
OMMS is the board game that everybody already owns.
It uses an everyday map as the board, coins, playing cards and matches for counters and cards.  Every game is different, because you can just try out a different map.
The aim is to defeat the other players by battling their units, sieging settlements and fortifications, and expanding your territories faster than they can.

Full rules available via the Web Link section near the bottom of this page

Classic battle game from World War II. Two enemy players fight on the opposite sides (the red and the blue bases).
The game includes a classic gameboard for the trench fighting, and 20-20 amazingly detailed hand painted army miniatures. Commanders deploy their soldiers through the fields, special terrains, and other objects.
The game mechanics are quite simple. Dice rolling movement, and special fields. (POW camps, Hospitals)
Average game length is between 30 and 60 minutes.

The title translates as "Get up! Get up, valiants!"

From the instruction sheet:

"BOGYMAN"

"A spine tingling board game of strategy and risk for 2-5 players"

"About the Game - The Bogy man has risen from the grave, stalking the town of Homewood by night, and hiding in the shadows of the old abandoned mansion by day. The only way to send the Bogyman back to the grave is to enter the old mansion, find the nine missing pieces to the magic medallion, and assemble it. Four brave town's people have accepted the challenge even though others before them have tried and never returned. Their chances for survival are very small, but that's their problem."

Contents include:
Instruction sheet 
Game board
28 cards
Five standard pawns

From the book cover:
"Card Games For Two offers a feast of card games with something suitable for all the family. From children's favorites such as Old Maid, through classic games like Whist and Rummy, to the exotically named Julepe and Klob, or the fiendishly competitive Bezique or Spite and Malice, this book has something for everyone. There are more than 100 games (including variations), fully described and graded for difficulty. Neither you nor your friends will want to be without a deck of cards again!"

An historical simulation of San Marino during the year of Italian Renaissance. Up to six players, that represents several factions of the country, fight in order to rule the small Republic. Each player controls a group of councilmen in the San Marino parliament (named "Arengo"). They fight each other in order to obtain more concessions and more adherents. However players have to act like a single person towards the numerous "external forces" that will try to destroy the small and rich Republic. The player that gain the most influence point win the game.

The game was produced in an edition of 492 copies.

The rulebook includes Italian and English rules.

description:
An electronic game simulating an NFL all-star football game where players key in offensive and defensive plays and listen to an electronic "announcer" for play results.  Game features star players from the late 1980s such as Joe Montana, Dan Marino, Jerry Rice, etc.  Offense and defense each have six formations (which are "announced" to both players upon keying-in), and each formation allows approximately 6-8 plays.

Game consists of a 3D plastic "stadium" game module with keypads for both players, instruction booklet, 2 play books, 12 formation cards, 34 player cards, and scoresheet.  Can be played by 2 players or solitaire against the computer.

From the Mesomorph Games website:

"Tapestry contains three attractive new strategy games for two or four players played with a unique set of stained wooden tiles and colored pawns.

In the first of the games, called Tapestry, players build the board as the game progresses, adding light and dark tiles, placing pawns to access regions, and sliding tiles to strategically alter the landscape and block opponents. When the board is complete, the player whose pawns access the most territory triumphs.

In Towers the board starts off full size but shrinks as players race to construct towers before bearing them off the board to earn points. It is tempting to try for the high-scoring tall or single-color towers but if you take too long or allow your towers to become isolated, your opponent may end the game before you are ready to score.

Conveyor is a strategic race game in which the object is to move pawns across the board, starting from your seating position. Each turn players also have an opportunity to alter the pattern of light and dark tiles by shifting a row or column. Players strive to be first to move all their pawns across the shifting landscape of the board.

Each of the three games presents different challenges and satisfactions lasting 30 to 60 minutes, and the unique tile distributions each time insure that no two games are ever the same. The Tapestry set includes 26 light-stained tiles, 26 dark-stained tiles, 8 red pawns, 8 yellow pawns, 4 white pawns, 4 black pawns, a cloth bag, and a rule booklet. The games are best played on a smooth surface such as a glass or wood table."

A free game given away in Dork Tower #25 and in the Dork Tower game.  It's Frag with characters (Ken, Carson, Igor, Matt, Kayleigh and Gilly) from Dork Tower. The board is the Pegasus Games store. The board, pieces and cards are paper and should probably be laminated before using.

description:
Sextett is a collection of 6 abstract strategy games packaged in one box.  According to the rules none of these games had been published before, although in fact at least a few are traditional games. Ravensburger catalog #12.026

The game box includes several sets of multi colored pawns used in all games and 3 double sided hard playing boards.

The games included are:

Quicki - a dice rolling race game where players move around an abstract grid of points trying to reach their colored places in the board center, players can be sent back to their starting places by other players choosing to land on their current position.

Ponte - from the translated rulebook: The object of the game is to reach the island by crossing streets and bridges with one figure as fast as possible and then build a tower and reach it by jumps.

The Magic Star - The object of the game is to try to move clockwise around the star on the outer or inner path beginning from the starting point.  The player who manages to get all of his game figures into his own star corner first wins the game.  A blockade figure constantly changes the aspect of the game.

Piggy Back - The object of the game is to get all the figures across the board first, whereby the players are forced at times to carry the opponent's figures "piggy-back" across the board or even to be carried in the same way themselves in order to reach their destination.  The point of the game is to try to take advantage of the piggy back rule whenever possible.

Fox Jumping - A gathering of foxes (2 or 4 fox families make 40 foxes) is disturbed by a hunting party.  Hastily each fox tried to hide in one of the 39 neighboring dens.  But since there is room for one fox in each den, the slowest fox loses the race.

Surakarta - This game originated in Indonesia.   It bears the name of the old time-honored city of Surakarta on the island of Middle Java.  The game is played on a grid of points with all points on the outer edge connected by a curved line that leads back to another board edge.  The idea is to move your pawns around the grid trying to get to a curved line that can be used to move to another side of the board and more importantly eliminate your opponents pawn.  The game is played actively in Indonesia by drawing lines in the ground and playing with pebbles.

boardgame: Full Thrust Fleet Book: Volume 2 (The Xeno Files)
description:
Full rules and ship design systems for three alien forces in the Full Thrust universe - the Kra’Vak, Sa’Vasku and Phalons, plus ship illustrations and stats for the navies of these three races.

Fleet Book 2 also includes some additional rules and updates to the general Full Thrust game system.

Miniatures rules for naval combat from 1930 to 1945.   Contains a sample game of the Battle of Denmark Strait.  Scenarios Battle of the Denmark Straits, Destroyer Duel, Hammer and Tong.   Contains fleet info for all major powers.

This ruleset is based on the discovery of the US Naval College "Gunfire Rules for Tactical Maneuver" a set of rules used for simulating fleet actions by War College Students.   These rules were introduced in 1922 and used through the Second World War at the College.  While much raw data and a good part of the information on correction factors were drawn directly from this document, distilling the thousand pages of rules, tables, charts and diagrams into a form that the average wargamer could use.

A version of the wargame rules used by the British Naval Staff Tactical Division in 1929 added additional ideas.

The result is this ruleset Battle Stations.   Gamescience published a boxed version of these rules titled Deluxe Battlestations.

The Gamescience version of the rules contained a basic ruleset added by Gamescience and came with the boxed deluxe set.   The Damian Housman Associates version does not contain a basic game.

Components:
Rule Book
Torpedo Mimic
Armor Penetration Table
Movement and Fire Sheet
Gunfire Results Computer
Master Dame Shee and Hit Record
100 Damage Results Cards

boardgame: Try Time: New Zealand's Rugby Trivia Game
description:
From the box:

"Try Time is the exciting new rugby trivia game for rugby enthusiasts. So you think you know your rugby? .... Try time will give you and your friends hours of fun and rugby banter - play the game 2 ways - collect categories, with the winner being the first to collect them all or set a time limit and accumulate points through tries, conversions and the occasional drop kick. Either way, a feast of fun awaits as you attempt to step your way to the line on New Zealand's foremost rugby trivia game."

CON! is a fast paced card game where players compete to the be most popular attendee at a generic convention.  Character cards are bid on as they come into play.  Each character has a set point value and changes the effects of the bids that are used to win the characters.  After each player has a chance to put a character into play, the hands refresh and new characters are dealt into play.

The bids consist of four suits: Stuff, Hobbies, Freakness and Tales.  Certain characters have different effects on the suits that maybe played.  Other cards Stops and Trumps allow to to eliminate rival player's cards or even knock them from the bidding entirely.  Hand management comes into play as your cards do no refresh until everyone has put a character into play.

Game takes approximately 10 minutes to play and is scalable, so the game takes the same amount of time regardless the number of players.  It is pure beer and pretzels and will have future expansions featuring more stereotypical con goers.  The game is best described as 1/3 luck, 1/3 strategy and 1/3 spite.

Secret agents (the players) walk around a casino, trying to protect an important politician. It also happens that one of the agents is a traitor, and is trying to kill him instead. Players may play roulette, black jack or dice to earn money to buy poisoned darts and paralyzing gas to attack other agents, or antidote and anti-gas masks to protect themselves.

This game is another cycling race game.

The race track is formed out of 15 cards which are laid one after another. There are 10 'incident' cards which are put face down on the table, and each player also has 14 movement cards, on which there are depicted 4 possible actions and one or more arrows.

The players choose a movement card simultaneously and put them together. The arrow(s) on the card of player 1 determine the actions player B has to take and vice-versa.

First to finish wins the game.

Originally released by Edizioni Giochi Lasco from Milan, 1952, this game was re-released in 1993 by Edizioni Lo Scarabeo from Torino.

Battleships at War: Atlantic is a stand-alone game covering WW2 tactical level ship-to-ship combat in the Atlantic, Med, and Baltic theaters. Includes sixty ships from five nations (Britain, Germany, France, Italy, USSR), rulebook, a dozen scenarios, Reference Card, and a Convoy module, in ziplock packaging. In the old-style Minden tradition, counters must be cut out prior to play. But, components are in COLOR, and game is fully compatible with The Mighty Hood game appearing in Panzerschreck #14. If you liked Graf Spee in Pzsk #13, my guess is you'll love both The Mighty Hood and Battleships at War: Atlantic.

From the publisher:

We are local business partners collecting various goods from the countryside and delivering them to Coastal Export Warehouses, Inc. CEW pays us in Lempira for everything we bring in plus bonuses for completing certain contracts. The pay is poor and the dangers many. If we are not avoiding snakes, chagas, flash floods and the like, we are watching out for robbers. So, why bother, you ask? It's a living and a lot of rural workers depend on us. If you lack the necessary pride, wisdom and courage, don't join us, join the banditos.

Your standard Monopoly with the theme surrounding the MCI Classic Golf Tournament. These were not made available to the public. There were only 5,000 copies made which makes it both rare and desirable to the Monopoly collector.

From the publisher:

Test your creativity by inventing terrifying tales that frighten, bewilder and electrify your fiends… ahem, friends. Your goal is to discard all of your tokens by seamlessly continuing the story using phrases on your Snippet Card. The player ahead of you just described a basement full of severed heads that moan for the return of their bodies. Now it’s your turn. You draw a card that says, “She ran screaming through the night into the woods.” How will you weave that phrase into your tale? Be quick and make sense! Take it to summer camp… take it on family outings… take it anywhere you want to send people scurrying into their sleeping bags! Includes 50 cards, 25 punch-out tokens and instructions. For 2 to 10 players.

Gameplay:

All the picture tokens (axe, noose, ghost, skull, etc.) are passed out and everyone draws a story snippet card. One person reads an intro card, and then players continue the tale incorporating the story snippets on their cards. If they mention an object on a picture token, they get to discard it. Other people can discard more than one token named during the storyteller's turn. If the storyteller doesn't make sense or takes too long, they must take a token from the discard pile. The first player to get rid of all their tokens is the winner.

boardgame: Spin the Bottle: The Game for Girls
description:
From the publisher:

It’s the game of blushes and crushes! Learn about yourself and your friends when you play this sensational slumber party game. You’ll answer fun, funny “what if” questions and collect pictures of the boys you want to kiss. Don’t worry, they’re not real boys. They’re just on cards! Maybe you’ll kiss Computer Curt or Shy Shawn. Only the spinner knows… Comes with a spinner, punch-out tokens, instructions and 50 cards. Give it a whirl!

From the publisher:

So much to do and so little time! Olivia hops onto her scooter and races to be first to jump up and down on her bed. A “pedal to the metal” game, with many unexpected twists and turns.

A board game simulation of the popular Net Ball game.

Described on the box as: "A fast moving game of skill and accuracy"

From the publisher:

Because YOU demanded it - Ground Combat in the Babylon 5 Universe is here! GROPOS (Ground Pounders) stand ready to begin their assault on outposts, cities, and planetary surfaces near you! Command tanks such as the Earth Odin, Minbari Chrystalis, Narn D'Va'Tren, and Centauri Hexus in your bid to overwhelm the enemy with raw, nail-biting firepower! Do anything a futuristic ground combat unit can do - call in orbital strikes, zip low over the ground in VTOLs, hide in dense urban areas, customize units with optional equipment and officers - and much more! The GROPOS Core Rules also serve as the entry point to the new MAXIM rules system, which will allow you to play ground battles in nearly any setting throughout history and beyond!

description:
From the publisher:

Universal Miniatures System
Along the endless road, war is a constant companion. Displaced civilizations from throughout time and imagination battle each other. Armies that have stepped from the pages of history face armies that could never have existed. This is the Way, an endless Road that the lost are drawn to, whether it is lost to history, to accident, or to imagination.

The Way of War is a universal miniatures system that allows players to control the kind of game they play. It allows everything from Napoleonic formations to wizards casting spells to vehicles running a muck to artillery of a most unusual nature.

The Way of War allows you to bring any model you want into the game, whether historical, fantasy, or just plain weird. The Way of War can incorporate any model, no matter how fantastic or with what special abilities, into the game.

Twenty armies are included, all new and different from what you've seen before.

The Way itself is a world in which any kind of battle that is possible to the imagination can be fought.

This is The Way of War. A miniatures game that allows you to do anything you can conceive.

This is merely a rulebook. You'll also need 10-sided dice, a ruler, and miniatures to play. Some useful templates are included on the last page of this book.

From the box :

"The most fascinating game you can play with friends. Take one card from each of the four categories: ethical, serious, surprising or intimidating disclosures. Are you willing to admit to your partners that the statement on one of your cards applies to you? If so, read the card out loud and win a token! Then for an additional token, try to guess who would be willing to admit what you just admitted. Nothing to confess? Take four new cards for your next turn. TOP SECRET - your guarantee of laughter, fun and excitement."

Consists in revealing shocking secrets about yourself and trying to find out who would also agree with you.

This game is no longer in production.
If replacement pieces are needed, please contact SimplyFun. We'll do our best to replace game pieces as long as they are available in our warehouse.

From the publisher:

"In Royal Toad, Prince and Princess cards are chosen from each player's hand to find their noble matches.  The player who holds on to the Royal Toad card before it "hops" off is the winner!"

A SimplyFun version of the card game "Old Maid," with the twist that players are trying to keep the "Royal Toad" instead of get rid of it.

Description from their website:

Debate This!™ is the hot new board game that celebrates the art of lively banter and creative embellishment. In a test of words and wit, players debate topics ranging from social norms to sports, pop culture to politics, relationships to personal behavior. During each round, teams square off to deliver the most clever, creative, entertaining and persuasive argument to support their position and win points from the independent judge. The judge likes to be amused, entertained and enlightened. More entertainment, more points. The first team to cross the finish line wins the game. But it's the debates that will have people talking the next day. When family and friends get together, Debate This!™ is the game that gives them all a chance to speak their mind.

"Brain Twister! is the only double-sided memory game in the world. Designed by master puzzle-maker Klaus Schröer, Brain Twister! offers a unique twist on the classic matching game: both the front and back images of each playing tile must be matched in order to score. Brain Twister! features educational and scientific artwork that will appeal to players of all ages."

Brain Twister offers a unique twist on the classic memory matching game.  The Discovery Channel version of the game features 90 double-sided tiles with fascinating images from across the universe including the Space Shuttle Endeavour, a remnant of a supernova, and the International Space Station.

From the Fantasy Flight Games Website:

"As if the return of the Dragon Lords wasn't bad enough, Terrinoth is under assault by another enemy, if anything more devastating and more implacable. With the return of the Dragon Lords comes the return of one of their most ancient foes: the Giant Lords."

When 8 Giant Lords have been defeated, an special endgame scenario begins where giants invade and occupy a number of cities on board. Defeating these foes will be a challenge, as they can even use items from the marketplace of the occupied cities!

They conquered the land and established a line of kings that ruled the entire continent. In time, these kings became lazy, decadent ... and cruel. Eventually, the Elder Kings were overthrown, the cities became free, and then came the Dragon War and the Elder Kings were lost to the mists of history. Until now."

boardgame: A Little Golden Book I SEE YOU! Game
description:
From the publisher:

Shy Little Kitten and friends want to play "Peek-a-boo, I See You!", with your child. Place a character card in a sleeve and insert screens that progressively reveal more of the picture. Can you guess who's there?

From the publisher:

Thomas, Percy, Mavis and James are racing to see who can make three in a row! Rules for different skill-levels of play.

Contents: Game board, 20 engine tiles, instruction sheet for different skill-levels of play
family: TV Shows: Thomas the Tank Engine

description: The colour sets to collect are major cities around Japan. Instead of railway stations, there are complete railways. This seems to be a fairly straight translation of the more well-known game.

Roppongi Hills is a huge residential/business area in Tokyo that was opened in 2003. It includes a television station, galleries and museums, and very tall office towers. Instead of houses there are kiosks, and hotels are bigger retail shops ... and if you build enough shops, you get to build a Mori Tower.

(Mori was the businessman who spent billions buying up the land over a couple of decades - including the Australian Embassy, which was in a historic building which had an even more historic and beautiful garden.)

Ultraman is a creation of the 1960s and a huge part of popular Japanese culture. Various incarnations of Ultraman battle an assortment of weird monsters.
   The Ultraman Monopoly edition has Ultrapods instead of houses and Ultratowers instead of hotels.

The game consists of a battle between French and English troops. The army consists of artillery, cavalry and infantry who are used on a tiled board. Artillery is slow but powerful, cavalry is fast but weak and infantry are in between. Take care of the artillery because unguarded cannons can be taken over by the enemy!

By moving and advancing the troops one can attack the other (with dice).

description:
From the designer:

"The StapleWars board game is a game which combines action games, which involve moving parts (along the lines of Mousetrap or Weapons of War) and strategy games, which involve building and conquering, (like Risk or Axis and Allies) in one. All units and structures are made out of HEAVY DUTY staples, which can be found at any hardware store. Staplewars plays like a strategy game... until it is time to attack. Ammunition staples are flicked at units and structures, and if the units or structures are knocked down, they are destroyed! The purpose of this page is to encourage people to play this exciting game, to give people instructions for their own StapleWars arenas and sets, and to clarify information about this exciting new game. Give it a try, it is a lot of fun!"

From the rules:

Join KERMIT THE FROG, FOZZIE BEAR, MISS PIGGY and THE GREAT GONZO on a wild romp through the streets of London. Your favorite Muppet friends are in search of four very hard-to-capture thieves who are trying to steal a legendary jewel called the "Baseball Diamond". Join the Muppets on their adventure. If you're the first player to catch the four thieves and save the "Baseball Diamond", you'll win the game.

Here's some of the fun you'll have...take a cab and a bus, both of which are controlled by hidden wheels underneath the gameboard...be careful, though...if you spin the wheel too much or too little, you'll miss your bus or fail to hail a cab! Visit the Dubonnet Club with its mysterious spinning room and the Mallory Gallery where the "Baseball Diamond" is hidden. To enter the Gallery, you must make a precarious climb up the side of the Gallery Building. Then before you can save the jewel, you must be sure you own four different Thief cards. If you own the four cards, turn the hidden dial in the Gallery hoping that the jewel will be set free and come rolling out the front door of the Gallery (the jewel will be dislodged only when this hidden dial is spun a certain number of times). The player who frees the jewel from its hiding place wins the game. Have fun!

Collectible card game designed by Alexander Duncan, with roleplaying elements sold in starters and boosters. Artwork by Jamie Grant.

In 'Banemaster one of the players draws from the green deck and plays the evil Banemaster, the other players draw from the red deck and are sworn to fight the evil in the Banemaster's Castle.

During play, each player draws 8 cards from their respective decks: The Banemaster sets the location for his round and describes the setting. The other players go questing to defeat the Banemaster and his horde of monsters, while also trying to evade traps, collect treasure and score points. At the end of the round the Banemaster role rotates to the next player, and the game continues.

Trafikant (Swe.) or Commuter (UK) is a simple roll and move board game for 2-5 players about travel and road signs. Both versions has been adapted to national trafic rules and road signs etc. of the respectice countries, Sweden and the United Kingdom.

Players start at home and have to make five circuits of the board, representing journeys to work on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday.

Players take it in turns to move their (car) markers the number of space determined by the roll of dice.  The space that is landed on, many of which are marked with appropriate road signs, may affect the playing piece either in that round (go back to...) or in the next (throw 7 or more to proceed..).

Certain squares require a question on road safety or the rules of the road to answered.  These are graded into three levels to allow people of mixed ages to play.

Each circuit of the board earns a player a counter in his or her color.  The first player to receive five counters by completing his final journey with an exact roll of the dice is the winner.

The first version (the Swedish one) was made in partnership with Sweden's National Society for Road Safety.

Motala Strom is a unique 1-dimensional abstract strategy game where one tries to capture all of the opposing pieces, or having your very last piece standing in your home base.  The board consists simply of 9 spaces.  The game is named after a river in Sweden.

Online Play


    Super Duper Games

A trivia board game based on the popular UK ITV Soap "Coronation Street."

Features a board that looks like the famous street and includes a "who's who" booklet.

A children's game which is effectively a promotional item for Land Rover cars. The game is a simple roll and move game similar to Snakes and Ladders.  It comes with a laminated board, a set of rules, and a cut-out spinner and pieces.

The winner is the first player to reach the finish on an off road track, but you have to beware of hazards along the way, such as broken fan belts, mud, etc.

Kidnapped from your home world by decadent aliens, you have been transformed into a deadly cyborg warrior battling in star-crossed arenas for survival, glory, and, just perhaps, your freedom. You are one of the CyborGladiators!

This fast, fun, and furious tabletop fighting game includes rules for creating your own cyborgs with more than 30 unique cybernetic implants, earning glory in careers mode, and building an endless variety of arenas filled with dangers such as flamethrowers and pools of boiling acid for exceptional replay value. Featuring a cover by Bart Willard and interior art by Bart Willard and Richard Cox, the game includes a cardstock sheet of foldable color cyborg figures or you can play using your own action figures or miniatures. Ages 13 and up, no game master required

Expanded by:

    Invincible
Home Run Baseball Challenge is a two player baseball game where pitches, hits and fielding are determined by the rolls of custom dice and plays are planned using field action strategy cards.

The story is short: You move one of five well known characters from Springfield (Homer, Bart, Lisa, Flanders or Krusty) through the Springfield cemetery. The player who first finds three treasures is the winner.

It's possible to transform into a zombie or to steal treasures from your opponent.

Every character has two different attributes (normal and zombie). For example: Homer can move walls as a zombie and walk through walls when he is "normal."

A memory variant with pictures of Thomas the Tank Engine and friends.  Three different games are included: Station Stop Matching Game, Thomas & Friends Memory Game and Sir Topham Hatt's What's Missing? Memory Game.
family: TV Shows: Thomas the Tank Engine

boardgame: Serata in Giallo: Il Mistero del Nuraghe
description:
Serata in Giallo (Evening of Mystery) is the premier collection of murder mystery games to appear in Italy. The players are invited to an Evening of Mystery, during the course of which each player enacts the role of one of the protagonists of the event. The games unfold in “acts” with a theatrical flavor, and the game may or may not include an "investigation team."

Il Mistero del Nuraghe (The Mystery of the Nuraghe)

The “nuraghe,” as they are known, are mysterious ancient stone buildings that are found only in Sardinia…
Andrea Centofanti had been fine-tuning his personal theory that held that the nuraghe were none other than the “birthplace of fire." In order to verify his theory, he had decided to go to Sardinia to live for a few weeks inside a nuraghe.
     Four other people went with him, with the idea of spending a vacation that was a bit outside of the ordinary—without some of the comforts of home and without electric power.
     They thought they had prepared for everything, but none of them had counted on the tragedy that soon befalls them.  Angelo Corsini, one of the members of the expedition, dies in the blaze that he himself mysteriously creates in the nuraghe where he sleeps!

Place—Sardinia (Italy)

Time—1985

Characters—Five (3 men and 2 women):

Donatella Carta:  30-year-old professional assistant to a Milanese fashion designer. Girlfriend of Andrea Centofanti.

Andrea Centofanti: 35-year-old archaeologist. He is the boyfriend of Donatella Carta.

Fanny Corsini: 22-year-old aspiring actress. She is the girlfriend of Giovanni Lo Presti.

Giovanni Lo Presti: 25-year-old medical student. Fanny’s boyfriend.

Marisa Piccoli: 38-year-old athlete and actress. Married to a rich building contractor from Milan.

boardgame: Serata in Giallo: Luna di Miele sul Nilo
description:
Serata in Giallo (Evening of Mystery) is the premier collection of murder mystery games to appear in Italy. The players are invited to an Evening of Mystery, during the course of which each player enacts the role of one of the protagonists of the event. The games unfold in “acts” with a theatrical flavor, and the game may or may not include an "investigation team."

Luna di Miele sul Nilo (Honeymoon on the Nile)

The Excelsa, a luxury boat carrying a couple on their wedding cruise, floats slowly down the Nile in the direction of Cairo. On board is the famous “Dogon Wizard,” an illusionist who has the assignment of entertaining the passengers. But just when the show is nearing the apex of tension, something terrible happens.
The investigators must now perceive this situation as a typical "case" in the tradition of the "locked room": André Tavernier and his girlfriend come to their deaths in their suite which is dead-bolted from the inside. Who killed them, and above, all how? This is a homicide that only two persons can apparently have committed: the Dogon Wizard with his extraordinary paranormal powers, or the devil in person!

Place 
Egypt

Time 
June 1990

Characters
Seven—4 men and 3 women

Jean Giannetti (Dogon Wizard): 37 years old.  A fascinating man who is always surrounded by beautiful women.

Nando Restuccia: 30 years old.  An extroverted and talkative gymnastics teacher.

Pauline Tavernier Restuccia: 24 years old.  Most passionate about astrology and African history.

Marc Grun: 30-year-old journalist.  Specializes in winter sports.

Isabel Grun: 22 years old.  Completed her studies in America where she specialized in the History of Ancient Civilizations.

Mohammed: The Arabic guide.  A beautiful tawny boy of 26 years of age.  He always has a smile.

Patricia Breton: Science teacher from Marseilles. She is 33 years old. She is a woman with a lot of personality—level-headed and without delusions.

A CCG based on racing and fighting dragons.

From the website:

Dragon City is a place where the different colors of dragon are bred for sport, profit and industrial use. Different colors of draconium are purified and machined into various forms of gear and accessories designed to enhance dragon power. Dragon City is the place where the power struggles of the planet are played out on the vast Elite Class racing tracks. Here, mighty racers like modern day gladiators battle in 200 mile-per-hour competitions using glowing draconium gear and dragon energy in fierce hand to hand and dragon versus dragon struggles. In the deeper parts of the city, teens race and battle on the All City Racing Circuit hoping for a shot at the big time. Below in the depths, in a place called Squire’s End, dangerous Down City Crews wait, like street gangs, trying to fight their way up, not realizing that they are the remnants of the original empires that caused the first dragon-human war.

In Malhação, players try to discover each other preferences. Each one writes down their favorite song, movie, travel spot, etc. Then players arrange meetings on the board and exchange confidences. Some places have special powers, and each player can lie three times during the game, so knowing whether to trust or not an information is very important.

From the back of the box:

There's a bounty of learning contained in this high seas memory game where children match objects hidden in an adventurous tropical environment.

From the box:

A domino game.  In this simple game of observation, children lay brightly colored picture cards of cars, boats, trains, fire engines and helicopters on the table, next to other cards that show the same picture.  The first player to get rid of all his or her cards is the winner.

Ages 3 - 6

Alapo is a strategy game. Each player owns twelve abstract pieces, two each of six different kinds. Round pieces move in any of the eight directions on the 6 by 6 board; square pieces move only orthogonally and triangular pieces only diagonally. Large pieces move any distance, small pieces only one field per turn. 
Opponent pieces can be eliminated by moving onto their position. The goal is to reach the opponent's base line with one of your pieces without the opponent being able to eliminate your piece in his/her next move.

Bonus game included in Dragon Magazine #271.

Through Research, Development, and Fighting, you must improve your creature until he has a 6 or more in each of his body areas - 2 arms, 2 legs, heart and head.

Equipment: Pencil and Paper, six 6-sided dice, at least five 8-sided dice.

From the intro paragraph:

In Colossus, each player is a mad scientist building some sort of creature.  Perhaps it's a stone golem, perhaps it's a wicker man, perhaps it's a plain old laser-shootin' butt-kickin' giant robot.  Whatever the details, you're building them, and you like to make them fight.
family: Series: Chief Herman's Next Big Thing

description:
Bonus game from Dragon Magazine #273.

Cards can be discarded or used in a battle. When the draw deck is empty, the ship sinks, and there is a showdown to see who ends up in which lifeboat. Points are totaled for the hand, and multiple hands add up to a game target of 200.

A variation for 6-8 players and two decks is included.

Components are the rules and a "battle mat" depicting the pirate ship and lifeboats. Illustrations by John Kovalic.

From the intro paragraph:

The rats are long gone, but you're still here. You and your greedy pirate pals are hustling around the deck of a sinking pirate ship, trying to scoop up as much treasure as you can hold. You've got to have a little treasure to buy your way into a lifeboat, and the best lifeboats are the ones with the most treasure in them. It's kind of complicated. Then again, maybe you're just not very smart.
family: Series: Chief Herman's Next Big Thing

boardgame: Victory: The Blocks of War – Elite Blocksets
description:
The "Elite" blocksets for Victory: The Blocks of War add additional unit types, and are available in the same player colors as the base game and expansions. Red and blue are the original colors, but green, orange, gray, and black are also available. Note that each color of elite blockset is sold separately, so you'll need at least two elite sets (presumably a red elite, and a blue elite set each to start with) in order to allow play with elite units.

Like the original game's 50 units per side, each Elite blockset has 50 block units, but in 12 new unit types: elite fighters, torpedo bombers, medium bombers, destroyers, cruisers, elite armor, artillery, mechanized, mountain, engineers, marines, supply and resource units.

With the advent of Victory: World War II – Second Edition, elite blocks were included in the game's standard blockset.

Blocks of War - Elite blocksets

     #3526: Red Elite blockset - includes Twice if by Sea, a 2-player scenario
     #3527: Blue Elite blockset - includes Angel Run, a 2-player scenario
     #3528: Gray Elite blockset - includes Island Dodge, a 4-player scenario and special rules for Multi-Player games
     #3529: Green Elite blockset - includes The Big One, a 4-player scenario and special rules for Multi-Player games
     #3536: Orange Elite blockset
     #3537: Black Elite blockset


Expands:

    Victory: World War II

boardgame: Victory: The Blocks of War – Additional Blocksets
description:
Without expansion, Victory is a two-player game. The regular expansion blocksets for Victory each contain 50 wooden block units in any of 6 available colors: the red and blue of the base game, or orange, green, gray or black. Expansion blocksets come with a few simple additional rules for multi-player games. Victory 2nd ed. includes elite units in the game's standard blocksets and adds brown and yellow for a total of 8 available colors.

Blocks of War - WWII Standard Sets

     #3521: Red Blockset - Duplicate standard blockset
     #3522: Blue Blockset - Duplicate standard blockset 
     #3523: Gray Blockset - includes Canal Zone, a 4-player scenario and special rules for Multi-Player games
     #3524: Green Blockset - includes Centerpunch, a 4-player scenario and special rules for Multi-Player games
     #3531: Orange Blockset
     #3532: Black Blockset


Victory 2nd edition blocksets

boardgame: Victory: The Blocks of War – Logistics Set
description:
The Logistics set adds factories and other logistical issues to Victory.

Land, sea and air units each have their own factories, replacing the generic city production points of the standard game. Airfields can now be built, allowing air units to land even without a city or town. Mulberries are temporary ports that can be built by Marines. A storm affects a cluster of seven hexes and moves randomly each turn, preventing unit movement. All the logistics installations can be attacked and damaged or even destroyed, as can now bridges and canals, and appropriate markers are provided.

Expands:

    Victory: World War II

From the rules:

Grafolampo is a new, enthralling and instructive amusement, which has been invented for intelligent boys and for adults who are keen on puzzles.

Grafolampo is a competing cross-word, which leaves no ground to fortune but assigns victory to the promptest and shrewdest player.

The box includes:
4 game boards
300 letter cards
1 card distributor

In this game you are a pirate and with your ship you have to explore the archipelago, find the lost treasures and return to the bank on the main island.

Beware the other pirates who try to rob you, and also the many monsters you encounter on your way.

The first to collect 30 treasure points is the winner.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From the publisher:

"Tacos, spaghetti, and steak with ice cream.  Lay down cards that match either the tasty foods or cool colors.  No matching card?  Play a wild crazy #8 and name a new color!  With a bit of strategy and a pinch of luck, the first player to get rid of their cards wins!"

A SimplyFun version of the card game "Crazy Eights," using "oversized cards with engaging artwork" depicting various foods (instead of standard card suits). "Ideal for play dates and parties."

"Fish love to be with their friends!  Can you bring all the Chums back together?  Ask others for cards by number or go fishing to try and catch a pair of matching Chums.  The player with the most pairs at the end of the game wins."

A SimplyFun version of the card game "Go Fish".  Cards depict colorful fish.

Asalto al Poder is a tactical game for two players or teams who fight for the control of the capital city in a hypothetical state during a "Coup d'Etat". One of them controls the loyalist, the other the revolutionary forces. Combat is the last opcion, players try to atract the other's player counters to its side and win strategic locations.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
"A game of MONEY, POWER, and FUN For the OFFICALs and UNOFFICALS in and out fo the great state off TEXAS!

Dedicated to the FIGHTIN' and FUN LOVIN' members fo the TEXAS LEGISLATURE"  (direct quote from box cover)

A satirical game about collecting enough votes and cash to achieve office in Texas.  The bank in Sharpstown plays banker "under the table and from a brown bag," while another player (the election judge from Duvall County) tracks the winning conditions.  The rule sheet is half a page long, and does not include any instructions other than setting up and winning the game.  However, it appears to be a simple "roll the dice, react to square landed on" game while the players tour a linear board (the only path is to follow the outline of the state) until they can get enough votes and cash to reach the middle.

In Kubla's Gambol, both players start with one set of KublaCards and play them alternately next to one of the KublaCoins. When all cards have been played, the player with the higher total power next to it controls the coin. The player with the higher total value of controlled coins wins the game.

This was given to attendees of KublaCon 2005.

Off to See the Wizard Game - based on the animated TV series.

Players assume the roles of Dorothy and her friends, competing with each other, trying to capture the Witch and bring her to the Wizard.

The Game is played in two parts. The first part consists of chasing and capturing the Witch.

Player pieces travel on red circles and the Witch disc travels on yellow triangles. If a player piece and the Witch disc coincide on a black square the player captures the Witch.

The second part is getting the Witch to the Wizard.

The player with the Witch disc tries to get it to the Wizard while chased by the other players.

The Witch may exchange hands several times before one player succeeds in getting her to the Wizard to win the game.

This is a stats-based hockey simulation game that features many minor league and historical seasons.

Players are rated for ability to score, pass, and play defense.

Mirror Mania is a colorful, retro strategy puzzle game that challenges players to master the art of light reflection and spatial reasoning. Players try to trace paths through a randomized maze of mirrored blocks to predict where the beam—or rather, their line of sight—will end.

Using a periscope-like View Cube, players make educated guesses about which Target Peg they’ll see after a path of reflections. Get it right, and score points based on the Mirror Blocks and Pegs involved. Wrong guess? No score this time.

With variants for beginners and experts alike, Mirror Mania invites both logic and trickery—players can rotate or reposition blocks to block rivals or improve their own chances. Bright neon colors and psychedelic aesthetics make this a standout both visually and mentally.

Take the driver's seat in this sensational, brain-teasing game! Just follow the road from start to finish. Sounds simple…but can it be done using a reflected image? Whether alone, with friends, or at a party, Mirror Mania is sure to bring hours of fun, laughter and excitement.

Contents: Easy-to-Assemble Game Tray, Play sheets, 2 Felt Tip Pens, Instruction Sheet.

Follow the simple instructions for the easy-to-assemble game tray. Choose a play sheet and pen. Now you're ready to get behind the wheel! Observe the reflected image through the eye holes on the viewer panel. Place a pen at Start and go…Oops! Stay on the road! Fun to do, hilarious to watch. The eyes see an image the hand won't follow. Your mind says go, but your hand says no!

NFL Football simulation. Three colored dice for offense. Two more colored dice for special actions.

Roll the dice and see if you scored a touchdown or not.
The aim of the game is to invite nice relatives to your family feast, but watch out for the nasty ones (such as your mother in law)!

This game includes 60 cards; each with an answer on the top, number in the middle and a question on the bottom.

The cards bearing uneven numbers are divided among the men or boys. The even numbers are divided among the ladies or girls.

The player holding Number One card begins the game by reading the question on that card.

The player holding the Number Two card reads reads the answer at the top, getting up and making a courtesy as she does so. She then reads her question at the foot of the card, and Number Three reads his answer, standing up and bowing as he does so.

Proceed thus until all the cards are read. The answer to the question on card Number Sixty being read by Number One.

Per the rules: "The fun caused by the ridiculous questions and answers can be imagined when the game is played by a lively company."
Using two Donald Ducks, you flip cone shaped pieces to score on a numbered board...  Think Tiddly Winks meets Disney marketing.
Bible Eights is a Crazy Eights variant which uses Bible characters.

Bacchanales provides the step-by-step process, elements, and references required to accurately observe, smell, and taste a wine in order to determine the varietal, the vintage year, and even the appellation of a wine without having seen its label.  Detect the greatest number of clues and win the game.

Contents:
 ·  40 aroma reference vials
 ·  4 flavor reference vials
 ·  100 fill-in wine-tasting forms
 ·  2 wine-tasting guides
 ·  1 measuring spoon
 ·  1 glass Testaroma

Method:
The game unfolds in two phases: 1) Training players to recognize the aromas found in wine; 2) Observation and tasting of the wine itself.

1st Phase: Acquainting players with the aromas. This phase may constitute a full-fledged game in itself (see Variant below).  It is then a challenge of trying to guess the scent in each aroma vial (the answer is on the bottom).

These aromas are divided into 4 groups:
 ·  21 primary reference aromas (varietal aromas), ex: leather, licorice, apricot, etc....
 ·  7 secondary aromas (wine-making aromas), ex: cinnamon, lemon, etc....
 ·  7 tertiary aromas (aromas of aging), ex: prune, mushroom, etc....
 ·  5 odors indicating the principal defects found in wine, ex: detergent, cork, etc....

2nd Phase: The game itself. Each players has been asked to bring a bottle of wine.  All players have taken care to conceal the label from the other players.  The person whose wine is being tasted will temporarily be the game moderator while all others will try to answer the questions regarding the wine’s origin, vintage, grape varietal, defects, vinification process, aging potential, outcome, balance, etc.  Sounds difficult or impossible?  It’s not—and here’s why…

The accompanying wine-tasting guides provide all of the pertinent observations in wine-tasting.  The players’ challenge is to accurately match the observations of the wine you are tasting with the ones listed in the guides.  Alongside each observation you will find the interpretations or answers.  For example, if you observe a red wine with a ruby shade and a brick reflection, the interpretation will be a “2- to 5-year old” wine.  A Champagne or sparkling wine with small and fast bubbles is a “quality sparkling wine.”

Bacchanales was developed for wine-lovers of all levels, allowing participants to improve and perfect their knowledge in wine-tasting with each round.  On average, 80 percent of all people who have no prior wine experience can answer 3 out of 5 questions accurately.  In just one round, participants will become true connoisseurs, discovering the many secrets of wine-tasting.

Variant:
If your friends are Boardgame Geeks rather than wine-lovers, you can always forgo a regular game of Bacchanales to play a game of “Scent Identity.”  This variant using olfactory memory is original and very instructive: Randomly select 10 or 12 of the aroma vials (a greater number makes the game a little long and difficult). On a sheet of paper, draw a corresponding number of circles.  Label the circles with the names of the selected aromas.  Mix the aroma vials.  Set a time limit and have each player attempt to place the correct vial on the correct name!

Think Twice is played on two 6x6 grids each filled with the numbers 1-36 - each player fills one grid as they wish before the game starts.

On a turn a player selects one of the numbers and fills in the corresponding space on each grid. This continues until all the spaces are filled.

Players score for lines of four, five, and six in any direction. In addition, bonus points are awarded on each grid for the player with the largest connected group of spaces: they score the difference in size between this and the other player's largest group on that grid.

Archimedes is a nautical battle game for 2 players.

The board is an 8×8 grid, and each player has 10 (in the original version) or 12 "ships" represented by pieces of one color. The object of the game is to capture the opponent's ships.

Pieces may move any distance in any direction. They do not capture by moving; instead, a piece that could move to an opposing piece's space threatens it, and a piece threatened by at least three pieces may be captured.

A captured piece may be placed on the board as one's own piece instead of moving a piece. (The pieces need to be reversible or extra pieces need to be available to swap for.)

Online Play


    Super Duper Games

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

This is a Monopoly edition dedicated to one of the best Portuguese football (soccer) teams.

This German Trading Card Game is based on the weekly science fiction series Perry Rhodan. You you can play over the story from about the first 100 novellas issued. The game is broken up in different phases which you can play separately or one after another for a very long game. The phases with different backsides are:

Phase I: Die Dritte Macht (The Third Power, 1996)
Phase II/III: Topsider & Springer (1996)
Phase IV: Vorstoß nach Arkon (Approach to Arkon, 1997)
Phase V: Atlan & Arkon (2003)

Fanpro canceled the game in 1998. Finally Between The Stars (a company founded by fans of the game) took over and released phase V in 2003.

The player with the most victory points wins this complex game. You get victory points for completing phases and for completing side plots. Only after a phase is completed the players can draw cards from the next phase (you need separate decks for each phase you will play).

The cards show different planets, star systems, mutants with psi-talents, other persons, troops, spaceships, fleets, accessories, event cards and galactic riddles. There is also an action card deck to randomize the fights someway.

Every card can be "Pro", "Anti", or both ("Dual"). In a game you have to play two sides at the same time, the pro-side (the good guys) and the anti-side (the bad boys). After you have built up your fleets and armies (you normally hide them below other cards) you fight space fights and invasions on enemy planets: With your anti-side you attack the pro-sides of your opponents, and with your pro-side you damage their anti-sides.

Refight 3000 years of history covered by Archon 2nd Edition. Can you decisively change history by defeating Alexander the Great, Caesar, or Hannibal? Will the Greeks throw back the Persians or will Xerxes reign supreme? Fight through the centuries of East Asian warfare, or try your hand at the mysterious armies of Central America. Archon 2nd edition has been re-worked and re-tooled including extensive Campaign Musters. Be a leader, not a follower, and take charge of history!

Expands:

    Piquet: Master Rules for Wargaming

Cartouche is an eighteenth century supplement for the Piquet miniatures rules.  It covers such conflicts as the Wars of Marlborough, Seven Years War, and the American Revolutionary War.

family: History: War of the Austrian Succession
